answer:
**Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 20: "Ek Makra aur Makhi" (The Spider and the Fly)**[^1]

> [p-02078b] A spider to a fly one day did say,  
> “You travel daily down this very way,  
> 
> [p-558da0] But my poor hut has never been so blessed,  
> That you might stop and be my honored guest.  
> 
> [p-b934f8] To shun a stranger is no great disgrace,  
> But from your own, don't keep such distant space.  
> 
> [p-a3779c] If you would come, my honor would increase,  
> The winding stair is there, if you should please.”  
> 
> [p-088fa7] The fly replied when she had heard his plea,  
> “Good sir, go find a fool to trap, not me.  
> 
> [p-6cbf75] A fly will never come within your door,  
> For those who climb your stair are seen no more.”  
> 
> [p-7908b1] The spider cried, “Alas! You think me sly?  
> A greater fool than you, I've yet to spy.  
> 
> [p-79189f] I only wished to show you my respect,  
> There was no gain for me that I'd expect.  
> 
> [p-02eb12] You've flown so far, from where, God only knows,  
> What harm is there to rest here and repose?  
> 
> [p-0b1c0e] Inside my home are many things to see,  
> Though from outside a humble hut it be.  
> 
> [p-b6f251] Fine curtains hang upon the doors so thin,  
> And mirrors line the walls I have within.  
> 
> [p-513ce0] For guests, soft beds are ready and prepared,  
> A comfort that is not so widely shared.”  
> 
> [p-d9bebd] The fly replied, “That all may be quite true,  
> But don't expect that I will visit you.  
> 
> [p-5487cd] May God protect me from your beds so soft,  
> For those who sleep there are forever lost.”  
> 
> [p-7d0fa0] The spider thought, “She's clever, I can tell.  
> How can I trap this wretch within my cell?  
> 
> [p-1409dc] But flattery can make a hundred schemes succeed,  
> The world is slave to flattery's own creed.”  
> 
> [p-d0b552] And so he told the fly, “My dear lady,  
> God has bestowed on you great dignity.  
> 
> [p-17bb70] Whoever sees your face is filled with love,  
> A single glance is more than quite enough.  
> 
> [p-894882] Your eyes are like two diamonds, shining bright,  
> Your head, God crowned with a plume of light.  
> 
> [p-47641a] This grace, this dress, this beauty, and this art!  
> And then your song in flight that steals the heart!”  
> 
> [p-047eb6] The fly was softened by this flattering sound,  
> And said, “No cause for fear in you I've found.  
> 
> [p-a40222] To make refusal is a habit I deplore;  
> To break a heart—that's not what I am for.”  
> 
> [p-743773] She said these words and from her perch took flight;  
> The spider leaped and caught her in his might.  
> 
> [p-9576b5] He'd starved for days, a fact he couldn't conceal,  
> And made the little fly his easy meal.

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 20 "Ek Makra aur Makhi" [/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/20-ek-makra-aur-makhi/translation/](https://falsafa.corpus/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/20-ek-makra-aur-makhi/translation/). Translation by thothica. (24 stanzas, 411 words.)
